title: sqlite3.c:87338: int sqlite3VdbeExec(Vdbe *): Assertion `sqlite3BtreeCursorIsValid(pCrsr)' failed.
poc: CREATE TABLE v0(v1 UNIQUE, v2 UNIQUE);
CREATE VIEW v4 AS SELECT *FROM v0 WHERE v2 < 10 OR v1 < 7 ORDER BY v2;
SELECT '29' FROM v0 LEFT JOIN v4 ON NULL;
description:
yongheng added on 2020-02-22 04:18:54:
Consider the following poc:
---
CREATE TABLE v0(v1 UNIQUE, v2 UNIQUE);
INSERT INTO v0(v2) VALUES(0);
CREATE VIEW v4 AS SELECT *FROM v0 WHERE v2 < 10 OR v1 < 7 ORDER BY v2;
SELECT '29' FROM v0 LEFT JOIN v4 ON NULL;
---

It triggers an assertion on debug build of trunk.
drh added on 2020-02-22 13:07:53:
The optimization at check-in [9de3d7123007636a] allows a table that is
constrained by OR-connected terms to make use of a covering index.  It does
so by substituting the covering index cursor in place of the table cursor
when the table cursor is specified in the OP_Column opcode.
The optimization at check-in [41c27bc0ff1d3135] allows views or subqueries
to be the right-hand table of a LEFT JOIN.
At the intersection of these two optimizations was a case where a table cursor
was marked as NullRow but was then converted into a index cursor that lacked
the NullRow mark.  An assert() detected that the index cursor was not
positioned correctly.
The fix at check-in [f02030b3403d6773] blocks the substitution of the index
cursor in place of the table cursor when the table cursor is marked as NullRow.
key_bug_features: Substituting index cursor for NullRow marked table cursor
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE v0(v1 INTEGER UNIQUE, v2 TEXT UNIQUE);
CREATE VIEW v4 AS SELECT * FROM v0 WHERE v2 < '10' OR v1 < 7 ORDER BY v2;
SELECT '29' FROM v0 LEFT JOIN v4 ON v0.v1 IS NULL;
